description: Developers exploring biosensing in the lab, experimenting with OpenBCI and EmotiBit hardware during a session led by Yerzhan Zhamashev.
Lecture and discussion in the lab during a session on prototyping with microcontrollers, led by Karim Nicolae Costan.
Developers learning about FPGAs and hardware description languages during a session led by Daniyar Bakytuly.
Where ideas turn into prototypes. Desks filled with ongoing projects, screens showing code and schematics, and coffee cups fueling late-night debugging sessions.
A space designed for builders. Every corner tells a story of something being made, tested, broken, and rebuilt better. This is where experiments happen daily.
This is where the magic happens. Hardware meets software, theory meets practice, and strangers become collaborators. A space that runs on curiosity and caffeine.
The main collaborative area. Multiple projects happening simultaneously, with knowledge flowing freely between tables. Everyone is working on something different, yet somehow connected.
Our hardware library. From GPUs and robotics kits to sensors and dev boards, tools that would normally be out of reach are accessible to anyone with an idea.
Not everything happens at a desk. This is where demos turn into discussions, where people grab lunch between build sessions, and where some of the best ideas begin.
